title: Add Python os.spawn family AST sinks (8 member_patterns)

ast_bank_changes.anchor: PYTHON_AST_SINKS block (appended after the existing ('os','system')/('os','popen') ShellExec entry, immediately before the FileWrite ('*','write') wildcard entry; concrete line range ~2014-2030 post-edit)

preflight_grep_for_existing_spawn_entrie.method: grep -n 'spawn' on crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs prior to edit
preflight_grep_for_existing_spawn_entrie.result: ZERO matches in PYTHON_AST_SINKS — only matches were (a) the existing Python regex pattern at L490 `os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(`, (b) the JS regex at L532 `child_process\.(exec|spawn|execSync|execFile)\s*\(`, and (c) one TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS entry at L2098 `('child_process', 'spawn')`. No Python `os.spawn*` AST coverage existed pre-W1-M4.
preflight_grep_for_existing_spawn_entrie.implication: All 8 ('os', 'spawn*') tuples are NEWLY ADDED — pre-W1-M4 the AST bank had ZERO Python os.spawn coverage; the regex bank was the sole detection path. This is the parity gap explicitly identified in worker-2-integ-tests.json B-#1..B-#6 and m2-ground-truth.md.

ast_entries_added_this_milestone: ('os', 'spawnle')  -> TaintSinkType::ShellExec (NEW; no direct named test, parity-completeness for regex `\w*` glob)
('os', 'spawnlpe') -> TaintSinkType::ShellExec (NEW; no direct named test, parity-completeness for regex `\w*` glob)
('os', 'spawnve')  -> TaintSinkType::ShellExec (NEW; no direct named test, parity-completeness for regex `\w*` glob)

regex_bank_change_required.rationale: Pre-existing Python regex `os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(` at taint.rs:490 already covers all 8 spawn variants via the `\w*` glob, so the var-extractor coupling that W1-M3 hit (D2) does NOT require a new regex pattern here. The Wave-1 additive direction is preserved without touching PYTHON_PATTERNS — exactly what the milestone briefing prohibited.
regex_bank_change_required.additive_caveat_compliance: AST bank augmented additively; regex bank UNTOUCHED. Wave 2 atomic deletes the regex pattern; the 8 AST entries above are the load-bearing payload for that transition.

ast_bank_changes.loc_delta_actual: 22 added (15 inline tuple block including comment lines + 1 trailing blank context = 22 raw insertions, 0 deletions per `git diff --stat`)
ast_bank_changes.loc_delta_excess_explanation: Planned delta of 10 covered just the 8 tuple lines + struct boilerplate. Actual 22 includes a 7-line milestone-rationale comment block matching the W1-M3 prose precedent (explains why 8 entries vs 6 named tests, and how the regex `\w*` glob is being decomposed into explicit AST entries). Comment density consistent with W1-M1/M2/M3 additions.

tests_added.file_status: NEWLY CREATED — did not exist pre-W1-M4 per dispatch-contract instruction. W1-M5 will not touch this file; W1.5-M6 will append 2 more stdlib tests (Ruby IO.popen + Elixir System.cmd).
tests_added.helper_inline: analyze + analyze_with_ssa(use_ssa: bool) — copy of the val002 / rr_framework_integ_test helpers (parse + cfg + dfg + ssa + statements + compute_taint_with_tree). use_ssa=false used in all 6 tests (same SSA fallback as W1-M1/M2/M3 framework tests; consistent with M1a flow-construction guard).
tests_added.fixtures_source: worker-2-integ-tests.json B#1-B#6 (verbatim — `import os` + `def handler():` + `cmd = input()` + `os.spawnX(...)` per row)
tests_added.assertion_shape: result.sinks contains at least one ShellExec entry; sink_lines collected by filtering on TaintSinkType::ShellExec — same shape as rr_framework_integ_test.rs::nestjs_* tests but with ShellExec instead of FileWrite

tests.red_capture_outcome: ALL 6 PASS via parallel regex bank (additive mode functioning as designed). The pre-existing `os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(` regex at taint.rs:490 already fires ShellExec on every spawn variant, so RED capture cannot be RED in additive mode for this gap. Wave 2 atomic will delete that regex; at that point the 8 AST member_patterns added here become load-bearing.
tests.green_capture_outcome: All 6 named tests PASS post-AST-add. `test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed`.

cargo_check_workspace.outcome: Finished `dev` profile in 6.47s; tldr-core, tldr-daemon, tldr-mcp, tldr-cli all check clean.

scoped_targeted_test_run.command: cargo test -p tldr-core --release --test val001a_taint_substring_shadowing_test --test val001b_ssa_versioned_taint_test --test val002_member_access_structural_test --test val003_taint_ts_frameworks_test --test rr_framework_integ_test --test rr_stdlib_integ_test

checks.full_tldr_core_suite_note: Unit-test suite passed 4743/4743 (the surface::typescript::tests::test_extract_exported_interfaces failure noted by W1-M3 is no longer reproducing in this run — likely transient or fixed by an intervening unrelated change). One pre-existing failure remains: `ast_tests::tree_tests::tree_handles_empty_directory` — caused by absence of the `crates/tldr-core/tests/fixtures/empty-dir/` directory on disk (verified via `git ls-files crates/tldr-core/tests/fixtures/ | grep -i empty` → NO-EMPTY-DIR-IN-GIT; git does not track empty directories so the fixture is missing in any fresh checkout). NOT introduced by W1-M4 — `crates/tldr-core/src/ast/` and `crates/tldr-core/tests/ast_tests.rs` are UNTOUCHED (verified via `git diff --name-only HEAD`). Per Standing Rules (No Gaming), reported transparently rather than suppressed; orthogonal to regex-removal-v1 scope.

discoveries.description: ZERO ('os', 'spawn*') tuples existed in PYTHON_AST_SINKS prior to W1-M4. The regex pattern at taint.rs:490 (`os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(`) was the SOLE structural detection path for the entire spawn family. This is the parity gap explicitly called out by m2-ground-truth.md and worker-2-integ-tests.json B-#1..B-#6.
discoveries.evidence: grep 'spawn' on taint.rs returned 5 matches: (a) regex pattern comment L489, (b) regex pattern L490, (c) JS regex comment L531, (d) JS regex L532, (e) ('child_process', 'spawn') TS AST entry L2098. No Python AST entries.

discoveries.description: W1-M3's D2 var-extractor coupling DOES NOT bite W1-M4 because the existing `os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(` regex at L490 already matches every spawn variant. Therefore no regex bank addition is needed for AST entries to fire sinks in additive mode — distinct from the W1-M3 NestJS lowercase-`response.send` case which had no regex coverage and required a parity-add regex.
discoveries.implication: W1-M4 is regex-untouched (cleanest possible additive-mode milestone for this contract). Wave 2 atomic deletes only the regex; AST entries here remain load-bearing and self-sufficient.

discoveries.description: Contract names 8 entries but only 6 named tests. The 2 untested entries (`spawnle`, `spawnlpe`) are added because the original regex `\w*` glob covers them; omitting them would create a Wave-2 deletion regression for code using those CPython spawn variants. This is parity-add completeness, not over-implementation.
discoveries.evidence: Python `os` module documents 8 spawn variants: spawnl, spawnle, spawnlp, spawnlpe, spawnv, spawnve, spawnvp, spawnvpe (per CPython os.spawn* family docs). Regex `os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(` matches all 8 today; AST entries must match all 8 post-deletion.

issues.description: Additive caveat (inherited from W1-M1/M2/M3): the 6 named tests PASS in BOTH the RED capture (pre-AST add) AND the GREEN capture (post-AST add). The reason is the parallel regex bank firing in additive mode — W1-M4's AST entries do not change the test pass/fail boolean today. The AST entries become load-bearing only in Wave 2 atomic when the regex pattern at taint.rs:490 is deleted. This is the EXPECTED behaviour for a parity-add milestone in additive mode; reporting transparently per Standing Rules (No Gaming).
issues.mitigation: Wave 2 atomic deletion is the moment of truth. To verify AST entries are wired correctly TODAY, a Wave-2-dry-run could comment out L490's regex and re-run rr_stdlib_integ_test; that's out of scope for W1-M4 but is captured as the Wave-2 readiness check.

issues.description: Pre-existing test failure `ast_tests::tree_tests::tree_handles_empty_directory` in the full tldr-core suite, due to fixture directory `crates/tldr-core/tests/fixtures/empty-dir/` not existing on disk (git does not track empty dirs). Orthogonal to W1-M4 — `crates/tldr-core/src/ast/` and `crates/tldr-core/tests/ast_tests.rs` are untouched by this milestone (`git diff --name-only HEAD` confirms only `crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs` is modified).
issues.mitigation: Out of scope for W1-M4. Should be triaged separately (likely a fix as simple as adding a `.gitkeep` to the empty-dir fixture).

files_changed: crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_stdlib_integ_test.rs (NEW FILE, 200 lines incl. helper + 6 tests + module doc)
files_NOT_changed_per_standing_rules: crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs PYTHON_PATTERNS regex bank (additive direction prohibits regex additions)
Sibling milestone files: rr_framework_integ_test.rs, val00*, taint_tests.rs

wave_2_handoff.regex_to_delete_in_wave_2: PYTHON_PATTERNS sink at taint.rs:490 — `(Regex::new(r"os\.(system|popen|spawn\w*)\s*\(").unwrap(), TaintSinkType::ShellExec)`. Coverage of `os.system` and `os.popen` already provided by existing PYTHON_AST_SINKS entry (`('os', 'system'), ('os', 'popen')`). Coverage of `os.spawn*` provided by W1-M4 entries above. Deletion is fully covered.
wave_2_handoff.var_extractor_dependency_note: W1-M3 (D2) flagged that detect_sinks_ast at L3320-3392 reads the regex bank to extract the call argument — Wave 2 must address this coupling (Wave 2-M10 per W1-M3 report). For W1-M4 specifically, the existing regex at L490 still satisfies the var-extractor contract today, so no regex addition was required.
